title: citharexylum brachyanthum
intro: Citharexylum brachyanthum, also known as the 'Mountain Sage' or 'Cedar Sage', is a small shrub native to the tropical regions of Central America and Mexico, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits associated with Citharexylum brachyanthum include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. Citharexylum brachyanthum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
